operations.value: In a small crossover study, 200 mg of L-theanine attenuated the rise in blood pressure during psychological stress only among participants who showed a large blood-pressure response under placebo; this subgroup finding does not establish a general antihypertensive effect.
operations.notes: Participants were divided according to their blood-pressure response during placebo testing. The reported benefit was concentrated in the high-response group, whose placebo-condition systolic increases ranged from about 9.5 to 33.9 mmHg. The result should not be generalized to resting blood pressure or hypertension treatment.

sources.title: Effects of L-Theanine or Caffeine Intake on Changes in Blood Pressure under Physical and Psychological Stresses
Psychotropic Effects of L-Theanine and Its Clinical Properties: From the Management of Anxiety and Stress to a Potential Use in Schizophrenia

notes: Fifth controlled enrichment-pipeline patch. Adds a narrowly qualified cardiovascular stress-response finding without making a hypertension-treatment claim.
